children.heading: Multi-year plan
children.label: a Multi-year plan
children.text: Not later than 180 days after , the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of State, in consultation with the Director of National Intelligence, shall engage for the purposes of establishing a joint consultative mechanism with appropriate officials of Taiwan to develop and implement a multi-year plan to provide for the acquisition of appropriate defensive capabilities by Taiwan and to engage with Taiwan in a series of combined training, exercises, and planning activities consistent with the Taiwan Relations Act (; et seq.).December 23, 2022Public Law 96–822 U.S.C. 3301

children.label: A
children.text: allow Taiwan to respond effectively to aggression by the People’s Liberation Army or other actors from the People’s Republic of China; and

children.label: B
children.text: advance a strategy of denial, reduce the threat of conflict, thwart an invasion, and mitigate other risks to the United States and Taiwan.
An assessment of the relative priority assigned by appropriate departments and agencies of Taiwan to include its military to address such capability gaps and capacity shortfalls.
An explanation of the annual resources committed by Taiwan to address such capability gaps and capacity shortfalls.

A description and justification of the relative importance of overcoming each identified capability gap and capacity shortfall for deterring, delaying, or defeating military aggression by the People’s Republic of China;1So in original. The semicolon probably should be a period.1

children.label: A
children.text: the capability gaps and capacity shortfalls that could be addressed in a sufficient and timely manner by Taiwan; and

children.label: B
children.text: the capability gaps and capacity shortfalls that are unlikely to be addressed in a sufficient and timely manner solely by Taiwan.

children.label: A
children.text: the Foreign Military Financing, Foreign Military Sales, and Direct Commercial Sales programs of the Department of State;

children.label: B
children.text: Department of Defense security assistance authorized by chapter 16 of title 10;

children.label: C
children.text: Department of State training and education programs authorized by chapter 5 of part II of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ( et seq.);22 U.S.C. 2347

children.label: D
children.text: section 506 of the Foreign Assistance Act of 1961 ();22 U.S.C. 2318

children.label: E
children.text: the provision of excess defense articles pursuant to the requirements of the Arms Export Control Act ( et seq.); or22 U.S.C. 2751

children.label: F
children.text: any other authority available to the Secretary of Defense or the Secretary of State.

A description of United States or Taiwan engagement with other countries that could assist in addressing in a sufficient and timely manner the capability gaps and capacity shortfalls identified pursuant to paragraph (1).

children.label: A
children.text: table-top exercises and wargames that allow operational commands to improve joint and combined planning for contingencies involving a well-equipped adversary in a counter-intervention campaign;

children.label: B
children.text: joint and combined exercises that test the feasibility of counter-intervention strategies, develop interoperability across services, and develop the lethality and survivability of combined forces against a well-equipped adversary;

children.label: C
children.text: logistics exercises that test the feasibility of expeditionary logistics in an extended campaign with a well-equipped adversary;

children.label: D
children.text: service-to-service exercise programs that build functional mission skills for addressing challenges posed by a well-equipped adversary in a counter-intervention campaign; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other combined training, exercises, or planning with Taiwan’s military forces that the Secretary of Defense and Secretary of State consider relevant.

children.label: A
children.text: a list of defense articles of the United States that may be transferred to Taiwan during a crisis or conflict;

children.label: B
children.text: a list of authorities that may be used to provide expedited military assistance to Taiwan during a crisis or conflict;

children.label: i
children.text: the feasibility of employing such methods in different scenarios; and

children.label: ii
children.text: recommendations for improving the ability of the Armed Forces to deliver such assistance to Taiwan; and

children.label: D
children.text: an assessment of any challenges in providing such assistance to Taiwan in the event of a crisis or conflict and recommendations for addressing such challenges.

children.heading: Recurrence
children.label: c Recurrence
children.text: The joint consultative mechanism required in subsection (a) shall convene on a recurring basis and not less than annually.
